sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Emily Hernandez, a 20-year-old female, presents for yearly follow-up for asthma, allergic rhinitis, and food allergy.
She was switched from Symbicort to Wixela due to insurance changes.
She uses Zyrtec in the spring, summer, and fall for seasonal allergy symptoms, which work well for her.
She has a known peanut allergy, but tolerates almonds and cashews.
She has been trying to reduce her inhaler use in preparation for military enlistment.
She reports having tried to cut down on vaping and smoking, but still occasionally uses them.
Past medical history: Asthma, allergic rhinitis, food allergy (peanut), history of bronchitis, and asthma exacerbations.
Review of systems: Denies fever, chills, or other systemic symptoms.
Denies urgent care or ER visits for breathing issues.
Current medications: Wixela (250/50) 1 puff in the morning and 1 puff at night, 3-4 days a week; Albuterol (as needed); Zyrtec (over-the-counter); Epipen (refills).
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: Ears: Some wax buildup on both sides.
Mouth: Normal.
Nose: Normal.
Lungs: Sounds terrific.
Test Results: Spirometry: Results not specified, but mentioned to be comparable to 2019 results.
Assessment / Problem List:

Assessment: Emily Hernandez, a 20-year-old female, presents with well-controlled asthma, allergic rhinitis, and food allergy, but is trying to reduce her inhaler use in preparation for military enlistment.
Problem list: Asthma (active); Allergic rhinitis (active); Food allergy (peanut) (active); History of bronchitis (past); Asthma exacerbations (past).
Plan:
I prescribed Alvesco 80 mcg, 2 puffs twice a day for asthma.
I will call in the prescription for her.
I will also renew her Albuterol and Epipen prescriptions.
I instructed her to continue taking Zyrtec as needed and to stay away from peanuts and tree nuts.
I will refill her Epipen.
I instructed her to take the Wixela off to the side, but not to throw it away, in case the Alvesco does not work well for her.
Follow-up: I will see Emily in four months to repeat her spirometry and assess her asthma control.
